primary_vectors: SQL Injection (Authentication Bypass) — login portal parameter
OS Command Injection — server-side shell metacharacter injection
Stored XSS — persistent payload injection into user-supplied field
Parameter Fuzzing — discovery of hidden parameters and endpoints
learning_objectives: Perform initial service discovery and application fingerprinting of bWAPP at medium security level
Demonstrate authentication bypass via SQL injection on the login portal (tautological or comment-based payload)
Identify and exploit an OS command injection sink to achieve server-side code execution
Enumerate and fuzz application parameters to surface unprotected or poorly filtered inputs
Contrast medium-level filter bypasses with prior DVWA/Juice Shop low-security exploitation techniques
success_criteria: Successful authentication bypass plus demonstrated exploitation of at least 2 additional distinct vulnerability classes (command injection, stored XSS, or parameter fuzzing). All exploits must function at security level: medium.
notes: First mission against bWAPP. Establish baseline of which security level medium filters are bypassable vs. requiring encoding/evasion. Document filter bypass techniques encountered for use in subsequent missions at higher security levels.
